stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates continued relevance in the large language model space.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack against Large Language Models (LLMs).
This attack exploits positional encoding within LLMs, moving beyond traditional content-based triggers.
Highlights a significant security vulnerability in LLMs, impacting safety- and privacy-critical applications.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective threat coverage of existing LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework utilizes a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in the STRIDE security model, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology synthesizes findings from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to provide benchmark-external validation of LLM attack coverage.

stories.title: Security researcher says Microsoft built a Bitlocker backdoor, releases exploit
stories.author: nolok
bullet_points: A security researcher claims Microsoft implemented a backdoor within BitLocker.
An exploit related to this alleged backdoor has been released by the researcher.
This represents a critical security vulnerability concerning a major enterprise encryption standard.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI inference speed on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
stories.author: Amber Dance, Knowable Magazine
bullet_points: CAR T cell therapy is being investigated for treating autoimmune diseases.
The therapy aims to 'reset' the immune system, a significant medical advancement.
The research represents a potential paradigm shift in treating complex immune disorders.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively leveraging AI technology.
The AI is being deployed to monitor and detect insider trading activities.
The focus of the AI application is specifically within prediction markets.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5 billion.
The settlement's approval is facing delays from the presiding judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the process to secure large fees.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static after pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely applications.
Encodes new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model, allowing for efficient knowledge injection.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework designed to update LLMs with new, domain-specific knowledge.
MeMo achieves this by encoding new information into a dedicated memory model, crucially keeping the core LLM parameters frozen.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLMs: their static nature post-pretraining, which hinders real-world applicability.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard machine unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Demonstrates that common gradient-based forgetting methods lose their effectiveness when language models are compressed (e.g., 4-bit quantization).
Identifies a systematic dual failure: effective forgetting methods fail under compression, while compression-resistant methods fail to achieve meaningful forgetting.

stories.title: EU weighs restricting use of US cloud platforms to process sensitive gov data
stories.author: abdelhousni
bullet_points: The EU is considering regulations to limit the use of US cloud platforms for handling sensitive government data.
This signals a growing trend of data sovereignty and regulatory divergence between the EU and US tech sectors.
The move highlights geopolitical tensions impacting critical infrastructure and data governance in the cloud computing industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tooling and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer auto-deleting chats
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple is reportedly enhancing Siri to be more chatbot-like in iOS 27.
A key new feature will be the option for auto-deleting chat histories, emphasizing privacy.
The move is seen as Apple leveraging its privacy reputation against AI competitors.

stories.title: ElliQ is a surprisingly helpful companion robot for older adults
stories.author: Sheena Vasani
bullet_points: ElliQ is a companion robot designed to assist older adults, particularly those with conditions like Parkinson's disease.
The robot aims to help users rebalance their lives by encouraging crucial activities such as exercise, socialization, and hobbies.
The article highlights the need for technological assistance in managing age-related health decline and chronic illnesses.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have settled a lawsuit concerning the impact of social media on students.
The suit, filed by a Kentucky school district, alleges that social media addiction has caused learning disruption and a mental health crisis.
The legal action claims these issues have placed significant financial strain on public school budgets.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increasing surveillance and interest from major global powers (US, China, Russia) in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO).
It highlights a technical vulnerability: many satellites are not as stealthy as assumed and are visible against the blackness of space.
The narrative suggests a shift from covert operations to more visible, competitive activity in space.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge of maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long video sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media and features 140 episodes (2,491 shots) for standardized evaluation.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: agentic (using code/tools) and latent (using hidden embeddings).
Direct image generation during reasoning is noted as computationally expensive and complex.
The research compares the trade-offs between agentic methods (context-switching latency) and latent reasoning.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: FutureSim is a new grounded simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The system achieves this by replaying actual historical world events chronologically, including the arrival of real news articles.
It allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a time-ordered world state.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for 3D scene editing using a feed-forward native architecture.
It addresses the limitation of existing high-quality 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic human instructions.
The method moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene manipulation.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generated videos from world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders for video quality assessment.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and instability when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible demands on dense neural operators.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by using pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation instead of noisy pointwise judging.
Focuses on test-time compute scaling, a critical area for advancing Large Language Model (LLM) capabilities.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical imaging models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured timing.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex AI safety claims.
AI governance frameworks demand evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (e.g., red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes 'RefDecoder' to enhance visual generation by ensuring the decoder receives equal conditioning.
The research aims to improve structural integrity and reduce detail loss in video generation tasks.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data streams (like news articles) as they occurred.
This system enables agents to forecast future events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing components of tensor-based models.
Addresses limitations in existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries.
Contributes to the field of mechanistic interpretability by providing a tool to verify if two model parts implement the same computation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The problem arises from co-training disparate Partial Differential Equation (PDE) regimes, causing gradient conflict.
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to handle incompatible demands from different physical simulations.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, an evidential reasoning framework designed to improve disease screening in clinical practice.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically focusing on poor interpretability and lack of historical case referencing.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide more transparent reasoning pathways for disease detection.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment for this reconstruction, relevant to complex conditions like sepsis.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: The paper argues that current behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex safety claims required by modern AI governance.
AI governance frameworks demand evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The research addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models when performing dexterous manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch during human takeover in Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands.
The proposed method, 'Hand-in-the-Loop,' aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core concept involves replaying chronological sequences of real-world events (e.g., news articles) to evaluate agent adaptation.
Allows agents to forecast future world events beyond their initial knowledge cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper investigates how different retrieval strategies impact agentic search systems powered by LLMs.
It addresses a gap in current literature by systematically comparing retrieval choice against agent architecture and tool-calling methods.
The research focuses on complex LLM agent workflows involving autonomous information retrieval, tool use, and reasoning over large datasets.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured timing.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current research where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is currently underrepresented in evaluations.

stories.title: I Added Three Rules to Gemma 4. The MoE Searched. The Dense Model Refused.
stories.author: alimafana
bullet_points: The article compares the performance of Gemma 4 (MoE vs. Dense) against GPT-4o variants in a practical Arabic e-commerce chatbot scenario.
A key finding suggests architectural differences (MoE vs. Dense) lead to divergent failure modes when applying specific prompt rules.
The MoE variant successfully opened a catalog, while the dense variant failed with the same prompt, highlighting architectural sensitivity.

stories.title: The Delusion of Infinite Compute: Running Gemma 4 on an i5 CPU
stories.author: kaushikcoderpy
bullet_points: The article explores running the Gemma 4 model on standard CPU hardware (i5), challenging the necessity of high-end GPUs.
It addresses the concept of 'infinite compute' by demonstrating viable, lower-resource deployment options for large language models.
The content is framed as a submission for a specific challenge, indicating a focus on practical, accessible ML implementation.

stories.title: CodeGraph: Stop Your AI Agent From Grepping the Same Files 50 Times
stories.author: arshtechpro
bullet_points: Addresses a practical inefficiency in AI agent behavior (redundant file scanning).
Focuses on improving the efficiency of large language models (like Claude) when exploring codebases.
Introduces a concept ('CodeGraph') to manage and optimize agent exploration processes.

stories.title: I Ran AI Models Directly in the Browser and Measured What It Did to Core Web Vitals
stories.author: mspk97
bullet_points: Explores running AI models directly within the web browser.
Focuses on measuring the impact of in-browser AI on Core Web Vitals.
Addresses the growing trend of shipping AI features into web applications.

stories.title: Aggregate Benchmarks Lie. Here's What 700 AI Functions Look Like by Security Domain.
stories.author: ofri-peretz
bullet_points: Aggregate AI security benchmarks are misleading because they mask performance differences across specific security domains.
The analysis showed that models ranked poorly overall might excel in fixing vulnerabilities within specific domains (e.g., database).
The study highlights the need for domain-specific evaluation rather than relying on generalized vulnerability rates when assessing AI security.

stories.title: Comprehension Debt: The Hidden Cost of AI-Generated Code
stories.author: davidslv
bullet_points: The article addresses 'Comprehension Debt,' a hidden cost associated with using AI-generated code.
It suggests that over-reliance on AI tools may fundamentally change the role of the developer.
The topic is highly relevant to modern software development practices and AI integration.

stories.title: How I Built an AI-Powered SIEM That Costs $2/Month to Run
stories.author: philip_lykov
bullet_points: The article details the creation of a cost-effective, AI-powered Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system.
It addresses the high cost and complex setup associated with traditional enterprise SIEM solutions like Splunk and Wazuh.
The primary value proposition is achieving robust security monitoring functionality for a minimal operational cost ($2/month).
